skill.name: d3js-visualization
skill.description: Build interactive data visualizations with D3.js, including reusable chart templates, data helpers, responsive examples, and implementation guidance.
skill.summary: Create custom D3.js charts, dashboards, and network visualizations with reusable examples and guidance.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: AI review found no evidence of shell execution, credential access, weak cryptography, or prompt injection. Most static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans, JavaScript template literals, D3 key functions, and visualization terminology. The main real concern is reusable tooltip code that inserts dynamic HTML, which can cause XSS if untrusted chart labels are passed without escaping.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic Tooltip HTML Can Render Untrusted Data
medium_findings.description: The reusable tooltip helper and examples call D3 html rendering with dynamic content. This is legitimate for rich chart labels, but it can become DOM XSS if consumers pass untrusted dataset values without escaping or sanitizing them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uses html insertion for tooltip content. The examples use generated data, so exploitability depends on downstream use with untrusted labels.

low_findings.title: External D3 CDN Dependency
low_findings.description: The documentation and examples load D3.js from a public HTTPS CDN. This is a normal quick-start pattern, but it creates availability and supply-chain dependency concerns for production deployments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URLs are visible script and resource links. They are official or educational resources, not evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command hits are Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals used for D3 examples. No shell execution API or Ruby backtick execution was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found browser JavaScript and documentation syntax, not shell invocation. Repository search found no child_process, spawn, exec, eval, curl, or wget use.

low_findings.title: Static Key and Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported sensitive key and weak cryptography matches refer to D3 data join keys, keyboard event keys, CSS, SVG paths, and force simulation terminology. No certificates, private keys, or crypto implementations were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are normal D3 or HTML example code. No evidence of credential material or weak hash algorithm use was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTML Rendering From Dynamic Chart Values
dangerous_patterns.description: Tooltip helpers use html rendering rather than text rendering. Consumers should escape labels or use text nodes when chart data can come from users or external systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and security relevant, but the bundled examples use generated local data. Risk rises when adapted to untrusted datasets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime Loading From External CDN
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use an external script URL for D3.js. Production users should pin versions, use integrity checks, or bundle dependencies locally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script tags are explicit and use HTTPS CDN URLs. This is not malicious, but it is a real dependency pattern.

content.value_statement: Custom charts often need more control than standard chart libraries provide. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build D3.js visualizations with reusable patterns, examples, and data helpers.
actual_capabilities: Guides D3.js chart setup with SVG, scales, axes, selections, and transitions
Provides reusable templates for line, bar, scatter, and pie style charts
Includes helpers for parsing, grouping, aggregating, cleaning, and generating data
Shows responsive chart patterns with resize handling and viewBox configuration
Demonstrates interactive examples for time series, bar charts, and network graphs
Covers integration guidance for common frontend frameworks and dashboards
limitations: Requires JavaScript and D3.js knowledge for production customization
Examples load D3 from a CDN unless users choose a local bundle
Tooltip examples need escaping when labels come from untrusted data
Does not provide a build system, package manifest, or automated tests

use_cases.title: Build Custom Product Analytics Charts
use_cases.description: Create tailored charts for product metrics when standard chart libraries cannot express the needed interaction or layout.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Dashboard Components
use_cases.description: Use reusable templates and examples to design responsive dashboard charts with tooltips, legends, and transitions.

use_cases.title: Visualize Relationship Networks
use_cases.description: Adapt force-directed graph examples to explore connections, communities, and node-level details in relationship data.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the D3.js visualization skill to create a responsive line chart for my time series data. Include axes, labels, and a tooltip.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Data for a Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to transform my CSV data into grouped totals for a D3 bar chart. Explain the required fields and chart structure.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interaction and Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my D3 chart plan and add hover states, keyboard access, ARIA labels, and responsive behavior. Avoid unsafe tooltip HTML.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Reusable Dashboard Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reusable chart pattern from this skill to design a configurable D3 component for a dashboard. Include update, resize, and cleanup behavior.

output_examples.input: Create a line chart for monthly revenue with tooltips and responsive sizing.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready D3 chart plan with dimensions, scale choices, axis labels, tooltip behavior, resize handling, and implementation notes.

output_examples.input: Show relationships between teams and shared projects as a network graph.
output_examples.output: A force-directed visualization approach with node groups, weighted links, drag behavior, zoom controls, legend guidance, and accessibility considerations.

output_examples.input: Turn raw sales records into a grouped quarterly bar chart.
output_examples.output: A data transformation workflow that groups records by quarter and category, then maps the result into a grouped or stacked bar chart.

best_practices: Choose the simplest chart type that answers the user question clearly
Use text rendering or escaping for untrusted labels before displaying tooltips
Clean up old SVG elements and debounce resize handlers during rerenders
anti_patterns: Do not use D3 for simple static charts when a smaller chart library is enough
Do not insert raw user-provided labels into tooltip HTML
Do not render thousands of SVG elements when Canvas or aggregation is needed

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create D3.js charts, dashboards, responsive visualizations, and interactive network graphs.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance and examples are suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it include working examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes line chart, bar chart, and network graph examples that can run in a browser.

faq.question: Does it require a build step?
faq.answer: No. The examples can run directly with a browser and the D3 CDN. Production apps may bundle D3 locally.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Escape untrusted labels before placing them in tooltip HTML or use text-only tooltip rendering.

faq.question: What skill level is expected?
faq.answer: Basic JavaScript knowledge is expected. The references also explain D3 fundamentals and common chart patterns.
